title: Seven-Period Panel of Post-1990 Developing Democratizers (1990–2022)
summary: This dataset constructs a balanced 7-period (1990–1994 through 2020–2022) country-period panel for 61 post-1990 developing democratizers — countries where V-Dem political regime transitioned from autocracy (regime ≤1) to democracy (regime ≥2) after 1985 and whose GDP PPP per capita was below $15,000 at transition (2017 international dollars). The panel integrates six primary data sources: (1) ILO SDG 1.3.1 via SDMX REST API provides social protection coverage rates for 300 countries from 2009–2023, serving as the key treatment variable; (2) V-Dem via Our World in Data CSVs supplies political regime classification (0–3 Regimes of the World scale) used for democratizer identification, plus continuous liberal democracy (v2x_libdem) and electoral democracy (v2x_polyarchy) indices as outcome variables; (3) SWIID v9.92 (April 2026) provides cross-nationally comparable disposable-income Gini coefficients with uncertainty bounds for 188 countries; (4) UNDP HDR25 mean years of schooling covers 204 countries from 1990–2023 as the primary education proxy; (5) World Bank SE.TER.ENRR gross tertiary enrollment rate supplements education coverage for 249 countries; (6) World Bank NY.GDP.PCAP.PP.KD GDP PPP per capita is used both as the democratizer eligibility filter and as a panel covariate. The 61 qualifying countries yield 425 country-period observations across 7 periods, with 61 countries having ≥3 periods. Key methodological features: within-country education variation is 3.25× larger in this extended 7-period panel (within-SD=1.045) versus a 2-period ILO-only panel (within-SD=0.321), directly validating the extended panel design for identifying education-democracy pathways. ILO SocProt data covers 104 of 425 country-period slots (2009+ years). Schema follows exp_sel_data_out format: each example has an input JSON (all predictors: socprot, gini_disp, education, educ_mys, educ_tertiary, within-country deviations e_E/e_G/e_S), output JSON (ldem, v2x_libdem, v2x_polyarchy), and metadata fields (country_code, country_name, period_id, transition_year, gdppc_at_transition, observation counts). Audit statistics in data_audit.json document per-period coverage, within-SD ratios, and case-country inclusion flags (Turkey: included, Thailand: included, Hungary: excluded — GDP at transition >$15k, Venezuela: excluded — pre-1985 democracy).
